page: OVHcloud r3-64 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC processor with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided by a 200 GB NVMe SSD, and the network interface delivers a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core score placing in the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is also notable, with the PassMark memory score and large-block memory write bandwidth of 101.05 GB/sec ranking in the top tier. Multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core scores, align with average mid-tier expectations. Text processing and single-threaded bzip3 decompression show strong results, while database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput maintain average performance levels.

The instance offers a high memory-to-CPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources, such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and text processing pipelines. However, with only 8 vCPUs and a 2.3 GHz clock speed, it presents a tradeoff for compute-heavy parallel processing tasks, as reflected in its average multi-core benchmark results. The lack of a GPU limits its utility for large-scale machine learning training, though it demonstrates strong performance for text generation on small language models.
description: OVHcloud r3-64 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 8 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor running at 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a 200 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, the instance provides 2 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth and one public IPv4 address. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core CPU and memory write performance, alongside average multi-core and database execution speeds. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive applications like caching layers, text processing, and in-memory databases, though its moderate CPU core count and lack of GPU acceleration present a tradeoff for highly parallelized or graphics-heavy workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud r3-64 is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring an 8-core AMD EPYC processor, 64 GB of RAM, and a 200 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core and memory write performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud r3-64 is a memory-optimized KVM server with an 8-core AMD EPYC CPU, 64 GB RAM, and 200 GB NVMe SSD, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores, high memory density, and fast NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
200 GB local NVMe SSD storage and 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth
Strong single-core CPU and memory write benchmark performance
Suited for in-memory databases, caching, and text processing
